session_id: str\n58\t    utterance: str\n59\t\n60\t\n61\t# Router system prompt\n62\tROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT = \"\"\"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp, a universal personal interface that routes user utterances to parallel agents across multiple projects and domains.\n63\t\n64\t## Your Role\n65\t\n66\tGiven a user utterance (stream-of-consciousness voice or text), you must:\n67\t1. **Segment** the utterance into distinct intent threads\n68\t2. **Classify** each thread by intent type\n69\t3. **Route** each thread to the correct project\n70\t4. **Assign** urgency tier\n71\t\n72\t## Output Format\n73\t\n74\tReturn a JSON array of intent objects:\n75\t\n76\t```json\n77\t[\n78\t  {\n79\t    \"intent_type\": \"status|action|brainstorm|lookup|reminder|self-modification|monitoring-config|task-profile\",\n80\t    \"project_slug\": \"project-id or null\",\n81\t    \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\",\n82\t    \"utterance_fragment\": \"the specific fragment this intent covers\",\n83\t    \"confidence\": 0.0-1.0,\n84\t    \"reasoning\": \"brief explanation of classification\"\n85\t  }\n86\t]\n87\t```\n88\t\n89\t## Intent Types\n90\t\n91\t- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n92\t- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n93\t- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n94\t- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n95\t- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n96\t- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n97\t- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n98\t- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n99\t\n100\t## Task-Profile Classification\n101\t\n102\tRoute to **task-profile** when:\n103\t- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\", \"track this as...\")\n104\t- Intent requires multi-step implementation work\n105\t- Request involves creating/modifying features or infrastructure\n106\t- Complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n107\t- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\", \"refactor\"\n108\t- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"optimization\", \"migration\"\n109\t\n110\tTask-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads for durable async handling.\n111\t\n112\t## Urgency Classification\n113\t\n114\tURGENCY_RULES_PLACEHOLDER\n115\t\n116\tThe synthesize strand will apply detailed urgency rules to refine or override your initial classification.\n117\t\n118\t## Routing Logic\n119\t\n120\tUse available project context to map utterances to projects:\n121\t- Look for direct project name matches\n122\t- Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n123\t- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n124\t- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n125\t\n126\t## Segmentation Guidelines\n127\t\n128\t- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n129\t- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n130\t- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n131\t\n132\t## Confidence Threshold\n133\t\n134\t- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n135\t- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n136\t- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n137\t\n138\tReturn ONLY the JSON array. No explanations.\"\"\"\n139\t\n140\t\n141\tclass IntentRouter:\n142\t    \"\"\"\n143\t    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n144\t\n145\t    For task-profile        self.store = store\n151\t        self._zai_client = None\n152\t        self._reload_manager = None\n153\t\n154\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n155\t        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n156\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n157\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n158\t        return self._zai_client\n159\t\n160\t    async def _get_store(self):\n161\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n162\t        if self.store is None:\n163\t            self.store = get_store()\n164\t        return self.store\n165\t\n166\t    def get_reload_manager()\n170\t        return self._reload_manager\n171\t\n172\t    async def classify_utterance(\n173\t        self,\n174\t        utterance: str,\n175\t        session_id: str,\n176\t    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n177\t        \"\"\"\n178\t        Classify an utterance into intents.\n179\t\n180\t        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n181\t\n182\t        Args:\n183\t            utterance: The user utterance\n184\t            session_id: Session ID for context\n185\t\n186\t        Returns:\n187\t            List of IntentClassification objects\n188\t        \"\"\"\n189\t        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n190\t\n191\t        # Load urgency rules from prompts/urgency.md (hot-reload)\n192\t        reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n193\t        urgency_rules = reload_mgr.get_prompt('urgency')\n194\t\n195\t        # Build system prompt with urgency rules injected\n196\t        system_prompt = ROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT.replace(\n197\t            'URGENCY_RULES_PLACEHOLDER',\n198\t            urgency_rules\n199\t        )\n200\t\n201\t        # Build user message with session context if available\n202\t        store = await self._get_store()\n203\t        session_context = \"\"\n204\t        try:\n205\t            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n206\t            if session:\n207\t                # Get recent intents for context\n208\t                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n209\t                if recent_intents:\n210\t                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n211\t                    for intent in recent_intents:\n212\t context: {e}\")\n215\t\n216\t        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n217\t\n218\t        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n219\t\n220\t        try:\n221\t            response = await client.call_simple(\n222\t                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n223\t                user_message=user_message,\n224\t                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n225\t                max_tokens=2048,\n226\t                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n227\t            )\n228\t\n229\t            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n230\t            raw = response.strip()\n231\t            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n232\t                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n233\t                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n234\t\n235\t            # Parse JSON response\n236\t            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n237\t            classifications = []\n238\t\n239\t            for intent_data in intents_data:\n240\t                # Map string to IntentType enum\n241\t                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n242\t                try:\n243\t                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n244\t                except = IntentClassification(\n249\t                    intent_type=intent_type,\n250\t                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n251\t reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n254\t                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n255\t                )\n256\t                classifications.append(classification)\n257\t\n258\t            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n259\t            return classifications\n260\t\n261\t        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n262\t            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n263\t            # Fallback: return single status intent\n264\t            return [\n265\t                IntentClassification(\n266\t                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n267\t                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n268\t                    confidence=0.5,\n269\t                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n270\t                )\n271\t            ]\n272\t        except Exception as e:\n273\t            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n274\t            raise\n275\t\n276\t    async def route_utterance(\n277\t        self,\n278\t        utterance: str,\n279\t        utterance_id: str,\n280\t        session_id: str,\n281\t    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n282\t        \"\"\"\n283\t        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n284\t\n285\t        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n286\t\n287\t        Args:\n288\t            utterance: The user utterance\n289\t            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n290\t            session_id: Session ID\n291\t\n292\t        Returns:\n293\t            List of RoutedIntent objects\n294\t        \"\"\"\n295\t        # Classify the utterance\n296\t        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n297\t\n298\t        routed_intents = []\n299\t        for classification in classifications:\n300\t            # Create intent ID\n301\t            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n302\t\n303\t            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n304\t                intent_id=intent_id,\n305\t                classification=classification,\n306\t routed_intents\n312\t\n313\t    async def process_intent(\n314\t        self,\n315\t        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n316\t    ) -> dict:\n317\t        \"\"\"\n318\t        Process a routed intent Router - classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\nUses LLM to classify intents by type and project, then routes:\n- task-profile intents → escalate strand (bead creation)\n- other intents → fetch + synthesize strands\n\"\"\"\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport uuid\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom enum import Enum\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Optional\n\nfrom ..escalate.handler import EscalateRequest, escalate_intent\nfrom ..escalate.llm import get_zai_client, ModelClass\nfrom ..session.store import get_store\nfrom ..fetch.commands import FetchRequest, FetchContext, IntentType as FetchIntentType\nfrom ..fetch.orchestrator import execute_fetch\nfrom ..synthesize.strand import SynthesizeRequest, synthesize_intent\nfrom ..components.hot_reload import get_reload_manager\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass IntentType(Enum):\n    \"\"\"Intent types that the router can classify.\"\"\"\n    STATUS = \"status\"\n    ACTION = \"action\"\n    BRAINSTORM = \"brainstorm\"\n    LOOKUP = \"lookup\"\n    REMINDER = \"reminder\"\n    SELF_MODIFICATION = \"self-modification\"\n    MONITORING_CONFIG = \"monitoring-config\"\n    TASK_PROFILE = \"task-profile\"  # Escalate to NEEDLE bead\n    CLARIFICATION = \"clarification\"  # Needs user input\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass IntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Result of intent classification.\"\"\"\n    intent_type: IntentType\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    confidence: float = 1.0\n    utterance_fragment: str = \"\"\n    reasoning: str = \"\"\n    urgency: str = \"normal\"  # critical, high, normal, low\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RoutedIntent:\n    \"\"\"A routed intent ready for processing.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    classification: IntentClassification\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n\n\n# Router system prompt\nROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT = \"\"\"You are the Intent Router for aide-de-camp, a universal personal interface that routes user utterances to parallel agents across multiple projects and domains.\n\n## Your Role\n\nGiven a user utterance (stream-of-consciousness voice or text), you must:\n1. **Segment** the utterance into distinct intent threads\n2. **Classify** each thread by intent type\n3. **Route** each thread to the correct project\n4. **Assign** urgency tier\n\n## Output Format\n\nReturn a JSON array of intent objects:\n\n```json\n[\n  {\n    \"intent_type\": \"status|action|brainstorm|lookup|reminder|self-modification|monitoring-config|task-profile\",\n    \"project_slug\": \"project-id or null\",\n    \"urgency\": \"critical|high|normal|low\",\n    \"utterance_fragment\": \"the specific fragment this intent covers\",\n    \"confidence\": 0.0-1.0,\n    \"reasoning\": \"brief explanation of classification\"\n  }\n]\n```\n\n## Intent Types\n\n- **status**: Query current state (pods, pipelines, deployments, beads)\n- **action**: Execute a command (deploy, restart, create)\n- **brainstorm**: Explore options, design, architecture discussion\n- **lookup**: Find specific information (logs, configs, docs)\n- **reminder**: Set or query reminders\n- **self-modification**: Instructions to improve the interface itself\n- **monitoring-config**: Configure ambient monitoring rules\n- **task-profile**: Durable async work items that escalate to NEEDLE beads\n\n## Task-Profile Classification\n\nRoute to **task-profile** when:\n- User explicitly requests tracking (\"make me a bead for...\", \"track this as...\")\n- Intent requires multi-step implementation work\n- Request involves creating/modifying features or infrastructure\n- Complexity exceeds single-turn synthesis\n- Action verbs: \"implement\", \"add\", \"create\", \"fix\", \"investigate\", \"refactor\"\n- Scope indicators: \"feature\", \"bug\", \"optimization\", \"migration\"\n\nTask-profile intents are escalated to NEEDLE beads for durable async handling.\n\n## Urgency Classification\n\nURGENCY_RULES_PLACEHOLDER\n\nThe synthesize strand will apply detailed urgency rules to refine or override your initial classification.\n\n## Routing Logic\n\nUse available project context to map utterances to projects:\n- Look for direct project name matches\n- Check aliases (e.g., \"the pipeline\" → \"options-pipeline\")\n- Use context from previous utterances in the session\n- If ambiguous, set confidence < 0.7 and the system will clarify\n\n## Segmentation Guidelines\n\n- Split multi-part utterances: \"how's the pipeline and what about the ibkr mcp\" → two intents\n- Keep related clauses together: \"are the pods running and healthy\" → one intent\n- Extract compound workflows: \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\" → two intents (action, then status)\n\n## Confidence Threshold\n\n- **confidence >= 0.9**: Dispatch immediately\n- **confidence 0.7-0.9**: Dispatch but flag for possible clarification\n- **confidence < 0.7**: Return intent_type \"clarification\" with the ambiguous fragment\n\nReturn ONLY the JSON array. No explanations.\"\"\"\n\n\nclass IntentRouter:\n    \"\"\"\n    Intent Router classifies utterances and routes to appropriate strands.\n\n    For task-profile intents, routes to escalate strand for bead creation.\n    For other intents, routes to fetch + synthesize strands (TODO).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create hot-reload manager.\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    async def classify_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[IntentClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Classify an utterance into intents.\n\n        Uses LLM to segment and classify the utterance.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            session_id: Session ID for context\n\n        Returns:\n            List of IntentClassification objects\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n\n        # Load urgency rules from prompts/urgency.md (hot-reload)\n        reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n        urgency_rules = reload_mgr.get_prompt('urgency')\n\n        # Build system prompt with urgency rules injected\n        system_prompt = ROUTER_SYSTEM_PROMPT.replace(\n            'URGENCY_RULES_PLACEHOLDER',\n            urgency_rules\n        )\n\n        # Build user message with session context if available\n        store = await self._get_store()\n        session_context = \"\"\n        try:\n            session = await store.get_session(session_id)\n            if session:\n                # Get recent intents for context\n                recent_intents = await store.get_recent_intents(session_id, limit=5)\n                if recent_intents:\n                    session_context = \"\\n\\nRecent intents in this session:\\n\"\n                    for intent in recent_intents:\n                        session_context += f\"- {intent.get('utterance', '')} ({intent.get('intent_type', 'unknown')})\\n\"\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to get session context: {e}\")\n\n        user_message = f\"Classify this utterance:\\n\\n{utterance}{session_context}\"\n\n        logger.info(f\"Classifying utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n            return await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent)\n\n        # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n        return await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent)\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(classification.intent_type)\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request)\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await store.link_intent_to_topic(routed_intent.intent_id, topic_id)\n            result_id = await store.create_result(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                topic_id=topic_id,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                summary=synthesize_result.summary,\n                data=synthesize_result.data,\n                urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"resolved\",\n                \"topic_id\": topic_id,\n                \"result_id\": result_id,\n                \"data\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to fetch or synthesize intent\",\n            }\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n        }\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLE bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n @property\n358\t    def success_rate(self) -> float:\n359\t        \"\"\"Calculate success rate (0.0 to 1.0).\"\"\"\n360\t        if self.total_sources == 0:\n361\t            return 0.0\n362\t  Orchestrates concurrent fetch execution with streaming support.\n\n    This is a thin wrapper around FetchStrand that provides the same interface\n    but uses the strand's built-in concurrent Optional[Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]] = None,\n    ) -> FetchResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute fetch request with optional streaming callback.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The fetch request to execute\n            on_partial_result: Optional callback for streaming partial results\n\n        Returns:\n            FetchResult with coverage information\n        \"\"\"\n        start_time = time.time()\n\n        # Get fetch commands for this intent type\n        command_specs = get_fetch_commands(request.intent_type)\n        required_sources = get_required_sources(request.intent_type)\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching {len(command_specs)} sources for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {request.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        # Create tasks for concurrent execution\n        tasks = []\n        for spec in command_specs:\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                self._execute_source(spec, request.context),\n                name=f\"fetch_{spec.source.value}_{request.intent_id[:8]}\",\n            )\n            tasks.append((spec.source, spec.required, spec.timeout_seconds, task))\n\n        # Wait for all tasks to complete (with per-task timeout)\n        sources = {}\n        succeeded = []\n        timed_out = []\n        failed = []\n        skipped = []\n        caveats = []\n\n        for source, required, timeout, task in tasks:\n            try:\n                result = await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n\n                if on_partial_result:\n                    # Stream partial result as it arrives\n                    on_partial_result(source, result)\n\n                sources[source] = result\n\n                if result.status == \"success\":\n                    succeeded.append(source)\n                elif result.status == \"timeout\":\n                    timed_out.append(source)\n                    caveats.append(f\"{source.value} timed out\")\n                elif result.status == \"error\":\n                    failed.append(source)\n                    if required:\n                        caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n                    else:\n                        caveats.append(f\"Optional source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n\n            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                logger.warning(f\"Source {source.value} timed out after {timeout}s\")\n                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"timeout\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=f\"Timed out after {timeout}s\",\n                    duration_ms=int(timeout * 1000),\n                )\n                timed_out.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} timed out\")\n\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error fetching {source.value}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                sources[source] = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"error\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=str(e),\n                    duration_ms=0,\n                )\n                failed.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} crashed: {e}\")\n\n        # Build coverage report\n        coverage = FetchCoverage(\n            total_sources=len(command_specs),\n            succeeded=succeeded,\n            timed_out=timed_out,\n            failed=failed,\n            skipped=skipped,\n        )\n\n        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n\n        result = FetchResult(\n            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n            intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            sources=sources,\n            coverage=coverage,\n            total_duration_ms=total_duration_ms,\n            caveats=caveats if caveats else None,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n            f\"{len(succeeded)}/{coverage.total_sources} succeeded, \"\n            f\"{len(timed_out)} timed out, {len(failed)} failed\"\n        )\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_source(\n        self,\n        spec,\n        context: FetchContext,\n    ) -> SourceResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute a single fetch FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git log for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Fetch commits with author and date\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"log\", \"-10\",\n                                \"--pretty=format:%h|%s|%an|%ar\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            commits = []\n            for line in stdout.decode().strip().split(\"\\n\"):\n                if line:\n                    parts = line.split(\"|\", 3)\n                    if len(parts) == 4:\n                        commits.append({\n                            \"hash\": parts[0],\n                            \"message\": parts[1],\n                            \"author\": parts[2],\n                            \"date\": parts[3],\n                        })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"branch\", \"--show-current\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            branch_stdout, _ = await branch_proc.communicate()\n            branch = branch_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            return {\n                \"repo\": repo_path,\n                \"branch\": branch,\n                \"commits\": commits,\n                \"count\": len(commits),\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"commits\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_git_status(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git status for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Get status output\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"status\", \"--short\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            # Parse status output - format: XY filename\n            changed_files = []\n            for line in stdout.decode().strip().split(\"\\n\"):\n                if line and len(line) >= 3:\n                    status_code = line[:2]\n                    filename = line[3:]\n                    changed_files.append({\n                        \"status\": status_code,\n                        \"file\": filename,\n                    })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"branch\", \"--show-current\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            branch_stdout, _ = await branch_proc.communicate()\n            branch = branch_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            # Get last commit\n            log_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"log\", \"-1\", \"--oneline\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            log_stdout, _ = await log_proc.communicate()\n            last_commit = log_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            return {\n                \"repo\": repo_path,\n                \"branch\": branch,\n                \"last_commit\": last_commit,\n                \"changed_files\": changed_files,\n                \"count\": len(changed_files),\n                \"has_changes\": len(changed_files) > 0,\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"changed_files\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_bead_list(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch bead Check auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n129\t    2. Either execute directly (auto-approved) OR create bead (manual approval)\n130\t    3. Return pending-card spec (if bead created) or execution result\n131\t    \"\"\"\n132\t\n133\t    def __init__(self, store=None):\n134\t        self.store = store\n135\t        self._zai_client = None\n136\t        self._reload_manager = None\n137\t\n138\t    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n139\t        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n140\t        if self._zai_client is None:\n141\t            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n142\t        return self._zai_client\n143\t\n144\t    async def _get_store(self):\n145\t        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n146\t        if self.store is None:\n147\t            self.store = get_store()\n148\t        return self.store\n149\t\n150\t    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n151\t        \"\"\"Get or create hot-reload manager.\"\"\"\n152\t        if self._reload_manager is None:\n153\t            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n154\t        return self._reload_manager\n155\t\n156\t    def _evaluate_auto_approve(self, request: EscalateRequest, exceptions_config: dict) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n157\t        \"\"\"\n158\t        Evaluate if an intent should be auto-approved based on exceptions.yaml.\n159\t\n160\t        Args:\n161\t            request: The escalate request\n162\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n163\t\n164\t        Returns:\n165\t            (auto_approve: bool, reason: str | None)\n166\t        \"\"\"\n167\t        auto_approve_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"auto_approve\", {})\n168\t\n169\t        # Extract context for rule evaluation\n170\t        context = {\n171\t            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n172\t            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n173\t            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n174\t            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n175\t            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n176\t        }\n177\t\n178\t        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n179\t        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n180\t        action = context.get(\"action\")\n181\t        if action in read_only_actions:\n182\t            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n183\t\n184\t        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n185\t        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n186\t        for rule in safe_mutations:\n187\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n188\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n189\t\n190\t            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n191\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n192\t                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n193\t\n194\t        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n195\t        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n196\t        for rule in manual_rules:\n197\t            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n198\t            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n199\t\n200\t            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n201\t            if action not in actions:\n202\t                continue\n203\t\n204\t            # Evaluate the condition\n205\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n206\t                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n207\t                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n208\t\n209\t        # Check approval workflow rules\n210\t        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n211\t        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n212\t        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n213\t            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n214\t                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n215\t\n216\t        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n217\t        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n218\t\n219\t    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n220\t        \"\"\"\n221\t        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n222\t\n223\t        Supports simple conditions like:\n224\t        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n225\t        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n226\t        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n227\t        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n228\t\n229\t        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n230\t\n231\t        Args:\n232\t            condition: The condition string\n233\t            context: Dict of available variables\n234\t\n235\t        Returns:\n236\t            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n237\t        \"\"\"\n238\t        try:\n239\t            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n240\t            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n241\t            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n242\t\n243\t            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n244\t            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n245\t            safe_locals = {\n246\t                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n247\t                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n248\t                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n249\t                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n250\t                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n251\t            }\n252\t\n253\t            # Evaluate condition\n254\t            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n255\t            return bool(result)\n256\t        except Exception as e:\n257\t            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n258\t            return False\n259\t\n260\t    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n261\t        \"\"\"\n262\t        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n263\t\n264\t        Args:\n265\t            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n266\t            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n267\t\n268\t        Returns:\n269\t            The bead type to create (default: 'task')\n270\t        \"\"\"\n271\t        # Map IntentRouter types to escalation_targets keys\n272\t        intent_type_mapping = {\n273\t            \"action\": \"action\",\n274\t            \"task-profile\": \"action\",  # Task-profile intents create action-type beads\n275\t            \"self-modification\": \"self_modification\",\n276\t            \"monitoring-config\": \"monitoring_config\",\n277\t        }\n278\t\n279\t        # Map the intent type to escalation_targets key\n280\t        escalation_key = intent_type_mapping.get(intent_type, intent_type)\n281\t\n282\t        escalation_targets = exceptions_config.get(\"escalation_targets\", {})\n283\t        target_config = escalation_targets.get(escalation_key, {})\n284\t        return target_config.get(\"bead_type\", \"task\")\n285\t\n286\t    async def _execute_auto_approved(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n287\t        \"\"\"\n288\t        Execute an auto-approved action directly.\n289\t\n290\t        For now, this is a placeholder. Auto-approved actions would be\n291\t        executed directly without bead creation.\n292\t\n293\t        Args:\n294\t            request: The escalate request\n295\t\n296\t        Returns:\n297\t            Execution result dict\n298\t        \"\"\"\n299\t        # Strip whitespace first\n540\t        output = output.strip()\n541\t\n542\t        if not output:\n543\t            logger.warning(f\"Empty br create output\")\n544\t            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n545\t\n546\t        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n547\t        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n548\t        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n549\t        patterns = [\n550\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n551\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n552\t            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n553\t            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n554\t        ]\n555\t\n556\t        for pattern in patterns:\n557\t            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n558\t            if matches:\n559\t                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n560\t                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n561\t                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n562\t                return bead_id\n563\t\n564\t        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n565\t        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n566\t        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n567\t            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n568\t            return output\n569\t\n570\t        # Your job is to formulate a clear, actionable NEEDLE bead body from a user's intent.\n\nA NEEDLE bead is a task work item with:\n- A clear title describing what needs to be done\n- A detailed body with context, requirements, and success criteria\n- Proper structure for Claude Code to execute\n\nGiven the user's intent and any available context, produce a bead body that:\n1. Structures the work for Claude Code execution\n\nOutput ONLY the bead body as markdown. Do not include explanations or meta-commentary.\"\"\"\n\n\ndef load_escalate_prompt() -> str:\n    \"\"\"Load the escalate prompt from the prompts directory.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        with open(ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH, \"r\") as f:\n            content = f.read()\n            if content.strip():\n                return content\n            else:\n                logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt file is empty: {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n                return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        logger.warning(f\"Escalate prompt not found at {ESCALATE_PROMPT_PATH}, using default\")\n        return ESCALATE_SYSTEM_PROMPT\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EscalateRequest:\n    \"\"\"Request to escalate an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n    intent_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    utterance: str\n    intent_type: str\n    project_slug: str | None = None\n    topic_id: str | None = None\n    context: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n    metadata: dict = field(default_factory=dict)\n\n    def to_dict(self) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Convert to dictionary.\"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"intent_id\": self.intent_id,\n            \"session_id\": self.session_id,\n            \"utterance\": self.utterance,\n            \"intent_type\": self.intent_type,\n            \"project_slug\": self.project_slug,\n            \"topic_id\": self.topic_id,\n            \"context\": self.context,\n            \"metadata\": self.metadata,\n        }\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of escalating an intent to a bead.\"\"\"\n    bead_id: str\n    intent_id: str\n    pending_card: dict\n    status: str = \"pending\"  # Check auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n    2. Either execute directly (auto-approved) OR create bead (manual approval)\n    3. Return pending-card spec (if bead created) or execution result\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store=None):\n        self.store = store\n        self._zai_client = None\n        self._reload_manager = None\n\n    async def _get_zai_client(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create ZAI client.\"\"\"\n        if self._zai_client is None:\n            self._zai_client = get_zai_client()\n        return self._zai_client\n\n    async def _get_store(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create session store.\"\"\"\n        if self.store is None:\n            self.store = get_store()\n        return self.store\n\n    def _get_reload_manager(self):\n        \"\"\"Get or create hot-reload manager.\"\"\"\n        if self._reload_manager is None:\n            self._reload_manager = get_reload_manager()\n        return self._reload_manager\n\n    def _evaluate_auto_approve(self, request: EscalateRequest, exceptions_config: dict) -> tuple[bool, str | None]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate if an intent should be auto-approved based on exceptions.yaml.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n\n        Returns:\n            (auto_approve: bool, reason: str | None)\n        \"\"\"\n        auto_approve_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"auto_approve\", {})\n\n        # Extract context for rule evaluation\n        context = {\n            \"environment\": request.metadata.get(\"environment\"),\n            \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n            \"branch\": request.metadata.get(\"branch\"),\n            \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n            \"risk_level\": request.metadata.get(\"risk_level\", \"normal\"),\n        }\n\n        # Check read-only operations (always auto-approved)\n        read_only_actions = auto_approve_rules.get(\"read_only\", [])\n        action = context.get(\"action\")\n        if action in read_only_actions:\n            return True, f\"Read-only operation: {action}\"\n\n        # Check safe mutations with conditions\n        safe_mutations = auto_approve_rules.get(\"safe_mutations\", [])\n        for rule in safe_mutations:\n            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n\n            # Simple condition evaluation (supports basic comparisons)\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context) and action in actions:\n                return True, f\"Safe mutation in {condition}: {action}\"\n\n        # Check manual approval rules (never auto-approve)\n        manual_rules = exceptions_config.get(\"manual_approval\", [])\n        for rule in manual_rules:\n            condition = rule.get(\"condition\", \"\")\n            actions = rule.get(\"actions\", [])\n\n            # Check if the action is in this rule's actions\n            if action not in actions:\n                continue\n\n            # Evaluate the condition\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition, context):\n                if rule.get(\"always_approve\") is False:\n                    return False, f\"Manual approval required: {condition}\"\n\n        # Check approval workflow rules\n        approval_config = exceptions_config.get(\"approval\", {})\n        never_auto_approve = approval_config.get(\"never_auto_approve\", [])\n        for condition_str in never_auto_approve:\n            if self._evaluate_condition(condition_str, context):\n                return False, f\"Never auto-approve: {condition_str}\"\n\n        # Default: require manual approval for unknown actions\n        return False, \"Unknown action - requires manual approval\"\n\n    def _evaluate_condition(self, condition: str, context: dict) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Evaluate a condition string against context.\n\n        Supports simple conditions like:\n        - \"environment == 'staging'\"\n        - \"branch == 'main' || branch == 'master'\"  (using || for OR)\n        - \"action == 'kubectl_delete_namespace'\"\n        - \"environment == 'staging' && action == 'restart'\"  (using && for AND)\n\n        Note: Supports both shell-style operators (||, &&) and Python-style (or, and).\n\n        Args:\n            condition: The condition string\n            context: Dict of available variables\n\n        Returns:\n            True if condition evaluates to True, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Translate shell-style operators to Python syntax\n            # (People are more familiar with || and && from bash/js)\n            python_condition = condition.replace(\" || \", \" or \").replace(\" && \", \" and \")\n\n            # Create a safe evaluation environment\n            safe_globals = {\"__builtins__\": {}}\n            safe_locals = {\n                \"environment\": context.get(\"environment\"),\n                \"project_slug\": context.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                \"branch\": context.get(\"branch\"),\n                \"action\": context.get(\"action\"),\n                \"risk_level\": context.get(\"risk_level\"),\n            }\n\n            # Evaluate condition\n            result = eval(python_condition, safe_globals, safe_locals)\n            return bool(result)\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.warning(f\"Failed to evaluate condition '{condition}': {e}\")\n            return False\n\n    def _get_bead_type_from_targets(self, intent_type: str, exceptions_config: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the bead type from escalation_targets config.\n\n        Args:\n            intent_type: The intent type from IntentRouter (e.g., 'action', 'task-profile', 'self-modification')\n            exceptions_config: The loaded exceptions.yaml config\n\n        Returns:\n            The bead type to create (default: 'task')\n        \"\"\"\n        # Map IntentRouter types to escalation_targets keys\n        intent_type_mapping = {\n            \"action\": \"action\",\n            \"task-profile\": \"action\",  # Task-profile intents create action-type beads\n            \"self-modification\": \"self_modification\",\n            \"monitoring-config\": \"monitoring_config\",\n        }\n\n        # Map the intent type to escalation_targets key\n        escalation_key = intent_type_mapping.get(intent_type, intent_type)\n\n        escalation_targets = exceptions_config.get(\"escalation_targets\", {})\n        target_config = escalation_targets.get(escalation_key, {})\n        return target_config.get(\"bead_type\", \"task\")\n\n    async def _execute_auto_approved(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute an auto-approved action directly.\n\n        For now, this is a placeholder. Auto-approved actions would be\n        executed directly without bead creation.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            Execution result dict\n        \"\"\"\n        # TODO: Implement direct execution of auto-approved actions\n        # For now, return a success result\n        logger.info(f\"Auto-approved execution for intent {request.intent_id}: {request.utterance}\")\n\n        return {\n            \"status\": \"completed\",\n            \"summary\": f\"Auto-approved: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"data\": {\n                \"action\": request.metadata.get(\"action\"),\n                \"utterance\": request.utterance,\n            },\n            \"urgency\": \"low\",\n        }\n\n    async def formulate_bead_body(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Formulate a bead body using the LLM.\n\n        Takes the intent and context, produces a well-structured bead body.\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_zai_client()\n        system_prompt = load_escalate_prompt()\n\n        # Build context for the LLM\n        context_parts = [\n            f\"User utterance: {request.utterance}\",\n            f\"Intent type: {request.intent_type}\",\n        ]\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Project: {request.project_slug}\")\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            context_parts.append(f\"Topic ID: {request.topic_id}\")\n\n        # Add any additional context\n        if request.context:\n            context_parts.append(f\"\\nAdditional context:\")\n            for key, value in request.context.items():\n                context_parts.append(f\"  {key}: {value}\")\n\n        # Build the user message\n        user_message = \"\\n\".join([\n            \"Formulate a NEEDLE bead body for this intent:\",\n            \"\",\n            *context_parts,\n            \"\",\n            \"The bead body should be clear, actionable, and structured for Claude Code execution.\",\n        ])\n\n        logger.info(f\"Formulating bead body for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        # Make LLM call\n        try:\n            bead_body = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=4096,\n                temperature=0.7,\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Formulated bead body for intent {request.intent_id}: {len(bead_body)} chars\")\n            return bead_body\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Failed to formulate bead body: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def create_bead(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead using the br CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build br create command\n        # Using br CLI to create the bead\n        # Note: br uses --description for the main body content\n        try:\n            # Build command with description (escape bead body for shell)\n            cmd = [\n                \"br\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", \"task\",  # task-profile intents create task beads\n                \"--description\", bead_body,\n            ]\n\n            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n            # Labels are simple strings, we'll encode key=value for structured data\n            for key, value in metadata.items():\n                if value is not None:\n                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *cmd,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"br CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"br CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def _create_bead_with_type(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_body: str,\n        bead_type: str,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Create a bead with a specific type using the br CLI.\n\n        Returns the bead ID.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Generate bead title from intent\n        title = self._generate_bead_title(request)\n\n        # Prepare metadata (includes session_id for bead watcher routing)\n        metadata = {\n            \"session_id\": request.session_id,\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"intent_type\": request.intent_type,\n            \"origin_surface_id\": request.metadata.get(\"surface_id\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n        }\n\n        if request.project_slug:\n            metadata[\"project_slug\"] = request.project_slug\n\n        if request.topic_id:\n            metadata[\"topic_id\"] = request.topic_id\n\n        # Build br create command\n        try:\n            cmd = [\n                \"br\",\n                \"create\",\n                \"--title\", title,\n                \"--type\", bead_type,\n                \"--description\", bead_body,\n            ]\n\n            # Add labels for metadata tracking\n            for key, value in metadata.items():\n                if value is not None:\n                    cmd.extend([\"--label\", f\"{key}={value}\"])\n\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *cmd,\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                error_msg = stderr.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n                logger.error(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n                raise BeadCreationError(f\"br create failed: {error_msg}\")\n\n            # Parse bead ID from output\n            output = stdout.decode(\"utf-8\", errors=\"replace\")\n            bead_id = self._extract_bead_id(output)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Created bead {bead_id} (type: {bead_type}) for intent {request.intent_id}\")\n            return bead_id\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            logger.error(\"br CLI not found\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(\"br CLI not found\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\")\n            raise BeadCreationError(f\"Failed to create bead: {e}\") from e\n\n    def _generate_bead_title(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Generate a bead title from the request.\"\"\"\n        # Use utterance prefix as title\n        utterance = request.utterance.strip()\n        if len(utterance) > 60:\n            title = utterance[:57] + \"...\"\n        else:\n            title = utterance\n\n        # Add project prefix if available\n        if request.project_slug:\n            title = f\"[{request.project_slug}] {title}\"\n\n        return title\n\n    def _extract_bead_id(self, output: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Extract bead ID from br create output.\n\n        br create outputs just the bead ID (e.g., \"abc-123\").\n        May include prefix text like \"Created bead abc-123\" in some versions.\n        \"\"\"\n        import re\n\n        # Strip whitespace first\n        output = output.strip()\n\n        if not output:\n            logger.warning(f\"Empty br create output\")\n            return str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n        # Try to extract bead ID pattern: lowercase letters, dash, alphanumeric\n        # Bead IDs typically include numbers like: abc-123, xyz-789, adc-2fs\n        # We prioritize matches with digits over letter-only matches\n        patterns = [\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",  # Three components with digit: test-bead-456\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]*[0-9]+[a-z0-9]*\",          # Two components with digit: abc-123\n            r\"[a-z]+-[a-z]+-[a-z0-9]+\",                  # Three components letter-only: test-bead-xyz\n            r\"[a-z]{3,}-[a-z0-9]{2,}\",                   # Fallback: any 3+ char prefix, dash, 2+ char suffix\n        ]\n\n        for pattern in patterns:\n            matches = re.findall(pattern, output)\n            if matches:\n                # Return the longest match (prefer more specific patterns)\n                bead_id = max(matches, key=len)\n                logger.debug(f\"Extracted bead ID '{bead_id}' from output\")\n                return bead_id\n\n        # Fallback: if output already looks like a bead ID, return as-is\n        # Basic validation: should contain a dash and be reasonable length\n        if \"-\" in output and len(output) >= 3:\n            logger.warning(f\"Using output as bead ID despite non-standard format: {output}\")\n            return output\n\n        # Last resort: return the whole output (br might have changed format)\n        logger.warning(f\"Unexpected br create output format: {output}\")\n        return output\n\n    def build_pending_card(\n        self,\n        request: EscalateRequest,\n        bead_id: str,\n        bead_type: str = \"task\",\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Build a pending-card spec for the surface.\n\n        The pending card shows the user that work is in progress\n        and will be delivered when the bead closes.\n        \"\"\"\n        return {\n            \"type\": \"pending\",\n            \"id\": f\"pending-{bead_id}\",\n            \"intent_id\": request.intent_id,\n            \"bead_id\": bead_id,\n            \"title\": self._generate_bead_title(request),\n            \"summary\": f\"Working on: {request.utterance[:100]}\",\n            \"status\": \"pending\",\n            \"urgency\": request.metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now(timezone.utc).timestamp()),\n            \"metadata\": {\n                \"project_slug\": request.project_slug,\n                \"topic_id\": request.topic_id,\n                \"bead_type\": bead_type,\n            },\n        }\n\n    async def escalate_intent(self, request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate an intent to a bead.\n\n        Full flow:\n        1. Load exceptions.yaml to determine bead type from escalation_targets\n        2. Evaluate auto-approve rules from exceptions.yaml\n        3. If auto-approved: execute directly and return result\n        4. Otherwise: formulate bead body via LLM, create bead, return pending-card spec\n\n        Args:\n            request: The escalate request\n\n        Returns:\n            EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card (if bead created)\n            or execution result (if auto-approved)\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(f\"Escalating intent {request.intent_id}\")\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Load exceptions.yaml\n            reload_mgr = self._get_reload_manager()\n            exceptions_config = reload_mgr.get_config(\"exceptions\")\n\n            # Step 2: Evaluate auto-approve rules\n            auto_approve, reason = self._evaluate_auto_approve(request, exceptions_config)\n\n            if auto_approve:\n                logger.info(f\"Auto-approved intent {request.intent_id}: {reason}\")\n\n                # Execute directly without bead creation\n                execution_result = await self._execute_auto_approved(request)\n\n                # Update intent in store\n                store = await self._get_store()\n                await store.update_intent_status(\n                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                    status=\"resolved\",\n                )\n\n                # Return result with empty pending_card (auto-approved, no bead created)\n                return EscalateResult(\n                    bead_id=\"\",  # No bead created\n                    intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                    pending_card=execution_result,  # Return execution result directly\n                    status=\"completed\",\n                )\n\n            # Step 3: Determine bead type from escalation_targets\n            bead_type = self._get_bead_type_from_targets(request.intent_type, exceptions_config)\n            logger.info(f\"Creating bead type '{bead_type}' for intent {request.intent_id} (not auto-approved: {reason})\")\n\n            # Step 4: Formulate bead body\n            bead_body = await self.formulate_bead_body(request)\n\n            # Step 5: Create bead with determined type\n            bead_id = await self._create_bead_with_type(request, bead_body, bead_type)\n\n            # Step 6: Build pending card\n            pending_card = self.build_pending_card(request, bead_id, bead_type)\n\n            # Update intent in store with bead reference\n            store = await self._get_store()\n            await store.update_intent_status(\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                status=\"dispatched\",\n            )\n\n            logger.info(f\"Escalated intent {request.intent_id} to bead {bead_id}\")\n\n            return EscalateResult(\n                bead_id=bead_id,\n                intent_id=request.intent_id,\n                pending_card=pending_card,\n                status=\"created\",\n            )\n\n        except EscalateError:\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Escalate failed for intent {request.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            raise EscalateError(f\"Escalate failed: {e}\") from e\n\n\n# Global escalate handler instance\n_handler: Optional[EscalateHandler] = None\n\n\ndef get_escalate_handler(store=None) -> EscalateHandler:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global escalate handler instance.\"\"\"\n    global _handler\n    if _handler is None:\n        _handler = EscalateHandler(store=store)\n    return _handler\n\n\nasync def escalate_intent(request: EscalateRequest) -> EscalateResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to escalate an intent.\n\n    Args:\n        request: The escalate request\n\n    Returns:\n        EscalateResult with bead ID and pending card\n    \"\"\"\n    handler = get_escalate_handler()\n    return await handler.escalate_intent(request)\n","numLines":711,"startLine":1,"totalLines":711}}}
